---
title: "Descriptive title of the study"
subtitle: "Optional concise subtitle"
author:
  - name: "Dr Abdul Mannan"
    affiliations:
      - name: "Bangor Haemophilia Centre, BCUHB"
date: today
abstract: |
  State the clinical or scientific question, methods, principal results and
  interpretation in the word limit required by the intended venue.
keywords: [haematology, reproducibility]
bibliography: ../references.bib
format:
  html:
    toc: true
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    geometry: margin=1in
    number-sections: true
  docx:
    number-sections: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

::: {.callout-warning}
## Before you start
Replace all illustrative text and verify every bibliography record live. A
citation key is not evidence that its corresponding DOI, journal details or
clinical claim is correct.
:::

## Introduction

Define the decision or knowledge gap, what is already known, and the precise
objective of this work. Cite verified sources with `[@citation-key]`.

## Methods

Describe design, setting, participants or data source, outcomes, analysis,
ethics/governance and reproducibility materials.

```{python}
#| label: fig-example
#| fig-cap: "Replace with a descriptive, publication-ready figure caption."
#| eval: false
# Place reproducible analysis code here. Use labels so text can refer to
# figures and tables automatically, for example: @fig-example.
```

## Results

Report findings in an order that matches the objective. Put interpretation in
the Discussion, and refer to numbered outputs rather than duplicating values.

## Discussion

Explain the principal findings, strengths, limitations, implications and the
next question. Distinguish data-supported conclusions from informed inference.

## Declarations

### Ethics approval

State the relevant approval, service evaluation / audit determination, or why it
was not required.

### Data and code availability

State what can be shared, where it is deposited, and any justified restrictions.

## References

::: {#refs}
:::
